data.body:
## Revised Approach (after plan review)

File-level exclusion is WRONG. RED markers identify a function boundary within a test file, not whole files to skip. Some RED-marked files have GREEN tests before the marker that must still run.

Revised approach: Extract the RED marker tolerance logic from `record-test-status.sh` (lines 119-496) into a shared library. Suite-engine calls this after a test fails to check if all failures are in the RED zone. If yes → TOLERATED (not counted as failure). If no → FAIL as normal.

Key functions to extract: `get_red_zone_line_number()`, `parse_failing_tests_from_output()`, `get_test_line_number()`, plus the tolerance decision logic.

CI sets `SUITE_RED_TOLERANCE=1` (or provides `.test-index` path) to enable RED marker tolerance in suite-engine. When unset, behavior is identical to today.
